publicationStmt.publisher: "Maryland, Register of Wills Records, 1629-1999." Images. FamilySearch. http://FamilySearch.org: 3 December 2024. Citing Prerogative Court. Hall of Records, Annapolis.

div.head: Robert Henly. Will.
div.p:
In the name of God Amen the 15th day of February in the year
of our Lord One Thousand and Six hundred and eighty three I Robert Henly of Py--con
in Charles County Gen. being sick of body but of good and perfect memory thankful
to almighty God & calling to rememberanve the uncertain Estate of this transitory
life that all flesh must yield unto Death when it shall please God to call
do make constitute ordain and Declare this to be my last will and testament
in manner and form following working and annulling by these oresents all
declared either by word or writing and this to be taken only for my last will &
testament & none other first being permitent and sorry from the bottom of my
heart for my sins past most humbly desiring foregiveness for the same
I give and commit my soul to almighty God my saviour & redeemer in whome?
and by the merits of Jesus Christ I trust & believe -- to be saved & to have
full
full -- and foregiveness of all my sins that my soul with my
body at the general day of resurrection shall rise again with joy &
through the merits of Christs Death & passions shall Profet and inherit the
kingdom of heaven performed for his elect and chosen and my body to be
buried in such place where it shall please my Executor gereafter named to
appoint and now for the sitting of my temporal Estate & such goods
chattles & debts as it hath please Almigty God for above my D-- to
bestowe upon me I do order give and dispose the same in manner &
form following (that is to say)

I-- I will that all those debts and duties that I owe in rightor consience to any
manner of person or persons whatsoever shall be well and truly contended &
paid within convenient time after my decease by my Executor hereafter named

Ite I do give and bequeath unto my beloved Grand Daughter Charity
Courte the Daughter of John and Charity Courte two negro girls the one named
Elizabeth the other Sarah to have & to hold the s.d two negro girls with their
future increase unto her the s.d Charity Courte and her heirs forever also I do
give and bequeath unto the said Charity Courte one one feather bed and furniture
with chair and other -- things fitting and convenient for the furnishing
of one Room with all & once one silvr bowle which I desire maybe sent to
England that the s.d bowle may turned into a silver tankard for her &
-- I do give and bequeath unto the said Charity Courte six cowes with
their future increase to her and her heirs

It. I do give and bequeath unto Thomas Harris the son of Thomas Harris one
one negro boy called Jupiter to serve him seven years after my decease & in case
the said negro boy shall run away & absent himself out of the s.d Thomas Harris
his service during the manner of time aforesaid the s.d Thomas Harris shall
receive? noe damange ag-- s.d negro boy for his -- running away or absenting
himself out of his servive during the term of time aforesai and at the end &
--ination
--ination of s.d sevent years servitute the aforesaid negro boy Jupiter to
be free and discharged from all manner of service to be claimed by any
manner of person or persons whatsoever

It. I do give and between unto Bethsheba Harris the Daughter of Thomas Harris
four sheepe and two cowes with their future increase into her & her heirs &
two caves with their future increase to her and her heirs forever?

It. I do give and bequeath unto Elizabeth Nichols two caves with their future increase
to her and her heirs forever and as for the rest of my Estate both real and personall
virtue? all my Land & tenements that I am possessed? wieth in the county ot
any other place whatsoever togther with my stock of cattle hogs sheep horses
& mares & all other goods & chattles whatsoever I do give and bequeath unto my
well beloved son in law John Courte -- & unto my well beloved Daughter
Charity his wife to have and to & to hold the s.d land & tenements with all the --
belongings unto the same unto them the said John Courte & Charity his wife
and the heirs of their bodies Lawfully begotten forever together with my stake?
of cattle hogs sheep horses & mares & all other goods & chattles whatsoever to them &
their heirs to be delivered amonst them forever & I do hereby ordaine & appoint
my well beloved son in law John Courte J-- to be the hole and sole Executor of this
my last will & testament In witness wheteof I have hereunto set my hand &
seal the day & year first above written

Signed sealed delivered & published
In the presence of us hereunder written
his
Edward Lee Richard R Tent? C-- Lomas
mark
his
Robert C. Henly
mark

On the back of the foregoing will was the following Endorsement (to will)
By virtue of a commission fromm the honorable C-- Henry Darnell and C-- William
Diggs Chief judges in yesya,emtry causes to me Directed bearing date the 28th
day
day of February last part I have called before me the witnesses within named
& by their oaths accordinng to commission form have proved the within written
will and testament & viz!, by the paths of Edward Lee Richard Gent. Cleborn?
Lomas and Margarett Maeyslis? this 8th day of March -- 168? and did then
likewise administer to the within named John Courte J-- the oath of Executor
By viture of the same commission I have also called before me John Fanning
and Thomas -- & given th-- their oaths as appraisers to the said
Estate of Robert Henly deceased as witness my hand and seal this 31st day
of March Anno Domino 1684.
9 1/2 Sides
James Tyler
